name: Logistics Pressure Consequence Atlas
summary: GM-facing atlas translating transport, chokepoints, storm risk, tariffs, prison labor, archive access, and magical anomalies into political and social outcomes.

consequences: missing-person cases become factional quickly
travel papers alter witness willingness
public disorder harms House prestige disproportionally
pressure_nodes.gm_use: Any delay on the road can become a status test, not just a travel setback.

consequences: scholarly advantage compounds socially
time-sensitive information can be procedurally slowed
books become prestige-laden political objects
pressure_nodes.gm_use: Archive clues should often be delayed, altered by copies, or priced socially.

consequences: accusations of magic misuse are coercive currency
public works reflect penal policy
poor travelers face severe vulnerability
pressure_nodes.gm_use: A route through Roaz changes the value of spellcasting and the cost of procedural mistakes.

consequences: town confidence can collapse before physical shortage begins
hostility to caravans is punished economically rather than militarily
merchants become local political actors
pressure_nodes.gm_use: Treat caravan arrival or absence as a world-state event.

consequences: harbor cities gain leverage
safe passage knowledge becomes tradable power
coastal rumors often carry exaggerated but actionable fear
pressure_nodes.gm_use: Sea travel decisions should reshape economy, urgency, and the credibility of distant witnesses.

consequences: entry denial creates black markets in certification
hospitality becomes conditional
artists and researchers enjoy asymmetric exemptions
pressure_nodes.gm_use: The price of entry should often be proof, sponsorship, or visible conformity.

pressure_nodes.node: Soreheim descent chains and bridge spans
consequences: work stoppages become civic crises
food and ore timing create labor leverage
unfinished expansion invites raid and sabotage narratives
pressure_nodes.gm_use: Vertical movement should change faction exposure and who can intervene in time.
